prompt: Objects placed in the HashSet are not found by contains(). What is the bug?
choices: Mutating b.val changes b's hashCode; HashSet looks in the wrong bucket and cannot find the object, returning false
equals() should compare object identity (==) not field values
set.contains() always returns false for non-String objects
explanation: After insertion, b is stored in the bucket for hash=5. Changing b.val to 10 changes hashCode() to 10. set.contains(b) looks in bucket 10 and finds nothing, returning false. This is why mutable objects should not be used as HashSet/HashMap keys.
